text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > DUI Offender Programs 61 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Hispanic/Latino Community Native American Community BRIDGEWAY COMMUNITY HEALTH OUTPATIENT CLINIC MENTAL HEALTH AND CHEMICAL DEPENDENCY TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient mental health services, inpatient and outpatient chemical dependency treatment, and substance use disorder counseling. Treatment incorporates individual, group, and family counseling. Additional assistance includes DUII services, adolescent services, corrections services, smoking cessation, and primary medical care. 750 Front Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Age 13 and older Hours: Intake hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Program hours vary Email (503) 363-2021 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGEWAY COMMUNITY HEALTH STAYTON OUTPATIENT CLINIC MENTAL HEALTH AND CHEMICAL DEPENDENCY TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Programs include outpatient mental health services, outpatient chemical dependency treatment, and substance use disorder counseling. Treatment incorporates individual, group, and family counseling. Additional assistance includes DUII services, adolescent services, corrections services, smoking cessation, and primary medical care. 631 N 1st Avenue Stayton, OR 97383 Eligibility: Age 13 and older Hours: Intake hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Program hours vary Email (971) 772-4309 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HUMAN DEVELOPMENT ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services related to drug and alcohol addiction. Services include: individual, family, and group counseling, structured outpatient treatment, and DUII court mandated rehabilitation. 2301 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 962-8800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN PUENTES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers supportive services to the Latino/a/e and Hispanic communality and non-English speakers seeking healing and recovery. Provides bilingual/bicultural alcohol and drug treatment and mental health services, case management, mentoring, access and referral to housing resources, continuing care when treatment is finished, and if necessary referrals for medical, psychiatric, and holistic. Treatment plans are individually tailored and offered by clinicians and staff that share clients' cultural background and language. Treatment address conditions like depression, anxiety, trauma, and other life stressors. Plans include behavioral health services such as Mental health care Group counseling, individual counseling, outpatient addiction treatment, recovery groups, peer support case management, DUII treatment. Eligibility: Individuals who are: -Latinx (monolingual/bilingual) -Adults (over 18 years of age) in need of mental health counseling, substance abuse and/or DUII treatment Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 546-9975 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers alcohol and drug treatment services through a network of providers.
